title: 阳光下的罪恶
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\117-阳光下的罪恶-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
Hercule Poirot discusses his investigative method with Inspector Cargraves and Inspector Colgate, explaining that he looks for patterns in expert crimes. He notes a similarity between the death of Alice Corey and the case of Nellie Parsons, specifically that the husbands in both instances had airtight alibis.

Later, Colonel Weston informs Poirot of his decision to hand the case over to Scotland Yard. Weston believes the investigation is linked to drug smuggling at Elves Bay, involving Horace Blatt. Weston posits that Blatt uses a boat with a red sail to transport drugs, potentially hiding them in the Fairy Cave on a rocky outcropping. He suggests the drugs might be exchanged via a sandwich box placed in a lady's swimming bag during lunch. Weston theorizes that Eleanor Marshall was killed because she witnessed Blatt hiding goods in the cave. While Poirot expresses doubt regarding the certainty of proving Blatt's guilt, he decides to take action by arranging a picnic.

Poirot persuades a hesitant group to join the outing to Dartmoor to lift their spirits, including Emily Bryce, the Redfuns, the Stephens-Lennon-Gardners, Rosamond Dane, Linda, and an enthusiastic Mr. Bratt. Major Barry refuses to attend. Although Rosamond Dunn initially intends to stay behind because Linda has a headache, Mr. Burt and Mr. Bratt insist she join the group. The party departs in three cars, with the Gardners and Poirot traveling together.
